raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint because Citi has repeatedly denied my billing dispute ( Case XXXX ) for a charge of {$870.00} from XXXX XXXX in XXXX, XXXX, without adequately evaluating the substance of my claim. The basis of my dispute is material misrepresentation and deceptive sales practices not buyer 's remorse or price dissatisfaction. 

While traveling in XXXX, I was brought to XXXX  XXXX and subjected to a high-pressure sales environment in which the merchant made verbal representations about the quality, authenticity, and value of a pendant ( silver setting with pink star XXXX XXXX that were material to my decision to purchase. I was pressured into signing paperwork and providing my passport before I had adequate opportunity to review the documentation. 

After leaving the store and reviewing the paperwork, I discovered it contained no objective or verifiable information regarding gem quality or grading, metal purity or certification, or any independent valuation basis. Comparable items a silver pendant with pink star sapphire are widely available on platforms like XXXX for under {$100.00}. I paid {$870.00}. 

When I contacted the merchant the following day to request a refund, they refused and instead attempted to upsell me on additional items, further demonstrating the predatory nature of the transaction. 
I filed a written dispute with Citi in XX/XX/XXXX, including supporting documentation. Citi denied the dispute citing the merchant 's invoices, signed paperwork, and a passport copy. I submitted a detailed written rebuttal on XX/XX/XXXX, explaining that authorization does not equal informed consent when material information is misrepresented or omitted, and that under Visa dispute rules a transaction can be authorized yet still invalid where consent was obtained through deceptive sales practices. Citi has not engaged with this argument and continues to uphold the denial based solely on the merchant 's refusal to issue a credit. 

The merchant 's refusal to refund is not a valid basis to close a FCBA dispute. Citi has an independent obligation under the Fair Credit Billing Act to investigate the merits of my claim. They have failed to do so. 

I am requesting the CFPB require Citi to conduct a proper review of this dispute under misrepresentation and deceptive sales practice standards, and to reverse the {$870.00} charge.
